tweets.text: This year the "summer melt-up" started earlier bc the exogenous shock that submerged the proverbial "inflated basketball" that is the AI-turbocharged US economy happened earlier than last year. Liberation Day was on Apr 2. Iran conflict started on Feb 28. Otherwise it's the same.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: Just like Liberation Day last year, “trade war + TACO + higher tariff rates across the board” was actually more bullish than no trade war at all. “Iran conflict + TACO + disrupted and destroyed oil supply” will prob be more bullish equities than no Iran conflict.

tweets.text: Tesla FSD on “Mad Max” mode: instead of queuing patiently to get off/onto an exit ramp of a highway, it would drive all the way to the front of the queue and squeeze into the ramp potentially holding up oncoming traffic behind it. 😂
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: Tesla will win on self-driving bc Elon has the balls to push the boundaries and do things that Waymo doesn’t. FSD has a “Mad Max” mode, which I’m pretty sure is trained on asshole NYC drivers, and is the only way a self-driven car isn’t bullied by human drivers in cities.

tweets.text: I like Marcuse, and I don’t think the Frankfurt School is the root of all evil. Were they wrong? Often. Were they interesting? Absolutely. They were trying to understand why industrial societies did not evolve the way Marxists expected. Even when their conclusions were flawed, the questions they asked about mass culture, technology, consumerism, conformity, and power remain worth engaging with.

tweets.text: AI is not coming for your job. AI is coming for the boring parts of your job. The parts you hate... The parts that drain you... If you let it take those, what remains is time for you to be CREATIVE.

tweets.text: An essential read for anyone following/investing in commodity markets/stocks.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Commodity markets tend to break when inventories get tight before a supply shock hits.

That may be what’s happening in energy today.

In "Could the Tanks Run Dry?", we explore:
• Tightening inventories
• Slowing shale growth
• Oil & LNG supply risks
• Commodity scarcity https://t.co/X32oU63QLU

tweets.text:
The numbers are correct per Bloomberg Intelligence (cited in Cerebras’ April 2026 S-1). They cover Cerebras’ addressable slice of the AI inference market — not the full global total — projected to grow from ~$66B (2025) to $292B (2029) at 45% CAGR. This outpaces training infrastructure (~20% CAGR).

Thank you for your thoughts anyway.

tweets.text:
I miss these good old days. You sit in a warehouse with hundreds of students for hours with just a pen and paper. 

You need intense concentration and stamina. You can go to the bathroom, but you better run to not waste time. Every minute matters. 

The exams are over 3 days. It’s psychological warfare. I remembered looking at all the people and panicking because I knew there were only 17 spots open. 

I don’t know if I could do it again. But I love the subject and I can imagine the staff saying out loud « the authority of science, you have 6 hours »
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
The philosophy entrance exam to ENS, France's most selective school and the one with the most Nobel Prizes per student worldwide. 

This year's subject: "The authority of science." 6 hours. Handwritten. https://t.co/QKQUM8uQWW
tweets.full_text:

tweets.text:
Cancel your Netflix. The best show on the internet is Codex spending 11+ hours trying to solve the Millennium Prize math problem Navier-Stokes with no tools. No mercy. Just a long-running agent staring into the mathematical void hahaha!

No doubt long-running agents are a reality. BTW, the Clay Mathematics Institute offers $1 million for a valid solution. So the question is how many tokens (in $) will it cost for an advanced AI to solve the problem?

tweets.text:
Because I have a 15 months old kid and I love to walk/bike everywhere (I’m from France) my favorites are Duboce Triangle, Lower Haight, the Panhandle, and Cole Valley because it’s walkable, quiet, beautiful, and conveniently close to everything: grocery stores, restaurants, parks. 

If you don’t care about walkability/parks then Pacific Heights or Russian Hill are awesome. Fillmore too. 

I find Dogpatch a bit soulless but it’s clean and nice and next to OAI office. 

TL;DR: it depends on your lifestyle. If you like to bike/surf everyday or prefer the nightlife action then it’s different neighborhood. 

https://t.co/UgudT7pkeZ
